title: Tasks — Multi-Step Task Management
description: The agent uses a TaskManager to track complex, multi-step work with dependencies. Unlike simple todos, tasks have types, statuses, and dependency chains that prevent out-of-order execution.

steps.content: Implement JWT authentication for our Spring Boot API: create the JWT utility, the security filter, and the security config.
steps.annotation: A feature with dependencies: the JWT utility must exist before the filter, and the filter before the config. Tasks model this.
Tasks are created with explicit dependency chains. jwt-filter depends on jwt-util, security-config depends on jwt-filter. The TaskManager enforces this order.

steps.content:
Task group created: 'Implement JWT Authentication' (4 tasks)
Ready to start: jwt-util (no dependencies)
Blocked: jwt-filter, security-config, test-auth
steps.annotation: The TaskManager identifies which tasks are ready (no unmet dependencies) and which are blocked. Only jwt-util can start.
The agent claims the first task. Status transitions: pending → in_progress → completed (or failed).
Task is now active. The agent proceeds to implement it.
Implementing the jwt-util task. The JwtUtil class handles token generation, extraction, and validation.
Implementation complete. Now the agent marks this task done.
Marking jwt-util as completed. This should unblock jwt-filter.

steps.content:
Task updated: jwt-util → completed
Unblocked: jwt-filter (all dependencies met)
steps.annotation: The TaskManager automatically checks dependency chains. jwt-filter is now ready to start because its only dependency (jwt-util) is completed.
The agent checks overall progress to plan its next move.

steps.content:
Implement JWT Authentication — 1/4 completed
  ✅ jwt-util: Create JwtUtil class [completed]
  ⬜ jwt-filter: Create JwtAuthenticationFilter [pending → ready]
  🔒 security-config: Create SecurityConfig [blocked by: jwt-filter]
  🔒 test-auth: Write authentication tests [blocked by: security-config]
steps.annotation: A clear view of progress. Tasks with dependencies provide structure that prevents the agent from jumping ahead or missing steps. The agent will now work on jwt-filter next.
